---
title: "2016 Subaru Outback Stereo Build"
description: "A technical writeup and reference guide for upgrading the factory Harman Kardon audio system in a 2016 Subaru Outback, including head unit replacement, amplifier integration, active front-stage tuning, subwoofer installation, and factory wiring quirks."
date: "2026-05-07"
categories: [hardware, audio, car-audio, Subaru, DIY, documentation]
image: ../images/subaru-audio.jpg
---

## Overview

This project documents the process of upgrading the factory Harman Kardon audio 
system in a 2016 Subaru Outback into a much more capable, tunable aftermarket 
system while retaining daily drivability and factory convenience features.

The build centers around a Sony XAV-AX4000 head unit, Maestro integration, a 
Kicker LX 1300.7 multi-channel amplifier, an active Morel front stage, rear fill, 
and a JL Audio 10W6v3 subwoofer in a sealed enclosure.

The goal was not just to make the system louder, but to build a clean, flexible, 
well-documented setup with proper crossover points, gain structure, signal 
routing, and enough tuning control to make the car sound genuinely excellent.

## System Components

### Source and Integration

- Sony XAV-AX4000 head unit
- Maestro integration for steering wheel controls and vehicle feature retention
- Factory Harman Kardon amplifier removed from the audio signal path
- Factory Subaru wiring retained where practical

### Amplification

- Kicker LX 1300.7 seven-channel amplifier
- Active front-stage configuration
- Rear door speakers on independent rear channels
- Dedicated subwoofer channel for sealed JL Audio subwoofer

### Speakers

- Morel Tempo Ultra 602 MKII component speakers
- Front tweeters run actively on AMP 1
- Front door woofers run actively on AMP 2
- Rear doors run as passive rear fill on AMP 3

### Subwoofer

- JL Audio 10W6v3-D4
- Sealed custom enclosure
- Tuned for clean, musical low-end support rather than maximum boom

## Wiring and Signal Routing

This build required working around several Subaru-specific quirks, especially 
because the vehicle originally had the Harman Kardon factory system.

### RCA Signal Routing

The final RCA routing uses the Sony head unit outputs to feed the Kicker amp:

- Front L/R RCA → AMP 1 input
- Rear L/R RCA → AMP 3 input
- Subwoofer RCA → SUB input

The Kicker amp is configured so that AMP 1 feeds the tweeters and also supplies 
signal to AMP 2 for the front door woofers. AMP 3 remains independent for rear 
fill, preserving useful front/rear fader control from the head unit.

### Factory Wiring

- AHH-7881 adapter used to connect amplifier outputs into the factory speaker wiring
- Adapter ordered with tinned ends for easier termination
- Factory Harman Kardon amp bypassed
- Subaru telematics/DCM bypass required to restore the factory tweeter path
- Factory speaker wiring reused where practical to avoid unnecessary rewiring through the doors

## Current Tuning Notes

### Front Tweeters — AMP 1

- High-pass filter: 3.2 kHz
- Slope: 24 dB/octave
- Filter type: Linkwitz-Riley

### Front Door Woofers — AMP 2

- High-pass filter: 60 Hz
- Slope: 12 dB/octave
- Low-pass filter: 3.2 kHz
- Slope: 24 dB/octave
- Filter type: Linkwitz-Riley

### Rear Doors — AMP 3

- High-pass filter: approximately 65 Hz
- Slope: 12 dB/octave
- Used primarily as rear fill

### Subwoofer

- Low-pass filter: 80 Hz
- Slope: 24 dB/octave
- Filter type: Linkwitz-Riley
- Subwoofer high-pass filter set as low as possible on the amp, around 10 Hz

## Lessons Learned

- The factory amp and DSP need to be fully understood before replacing them
- The telematics/DCM wiring can affect the tweeter path
- Harness adapters save time, but the pinouts still need to be verified
- Active front-stage tuning is worth the effort
- Crossover slopes and gain structure matter as much as hardware selection
- A good sealed 10-inch subwoofer is an excellent match for this wagon

## Why Document This?

There is plenty of Subaru audio information scattered across forums, product 
manuals, wiring diagrams, and installer notes, but very little of it is presented 
as a single coherent build path. This writeup captures the practical decisions, 
mistakes, fixes, and tuning notes from a real build so that future me — or the 
next person attempting a similar build — does not have to rediscover the same 
details from scratch.

## Current Status

✅ Head unit installed  
✅ Maestro integration working  
✅ Factory Harman Kardon amp bypassed  
✅ Kicker LX 1300.7 installed and configured  
✅ Morel front components running active  
✅ Rear fill operational  
✅ JL Audio 10W6v3 sealed subwoofer installed  
✅ Initial gain staging and crossover tuning complete  
✅ System sounding excellent in daily use  

🔧 *Next: under-seat amplifier cooling fan triggered by Sony remote turn-on lead.*
